sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) delight, satisfaction (Woblgefallen), Pro 14:35 הָיָה לְרָצֹון Isa 56:7 Jer 6:20 and עַל־רָצֹון Isa 60:7, to be pleasing, acceptable (to God), to be approved.- Exo 28:38, לְרָצֹון לָהֶם לִפְנֵי יְהֹוָה “to (conciliate) favour for them before Jehovah,” i.e. that they may be accepted of Jehovah, comp. Lev 22:1-33 20 , 21 19:5 , לִרְצֹונְכֶם “that ye may be accepted,” or, that your sacrifice may be accepted; Lev 22:19, 29 Lev 22:29 23:11 .

(2) a delight, that with which any one is delighted, Pro 11:1, 20 Pro 11:20 12:22 15:8 16:13 specially what is pleasing to God, Pro 10:32 Mal 2:13.

(3) will, pleasure, i.q. Ch. רְעוּת, pr. that which pleases any one, like the French tel est mon plaisir, Psa 40:9 103:21 . עָשָׂה כִרְצֹונֹו to do according to one’s will, Est 1:8 Dan 8:4 11:3 , 16 Dan 11:16followed by בְּ of pers. to treat any one as one pleases, Neh 9:24, 37 Est 9:5. Used of wicked pleasure and wantonness, Gen 49:6.

(4) goodwill, favour, as of a king, Pro 16:15 19:12 especially of God, Ps. 5:13 30:8 Isa 49:8, בְּעֵת רָצֹון in the time in which one may be received to favour. Meton. benefits, Psa 145:16 Deu 33:23.
